result.summary: Datadog exposes a regional REST HTTP API, a remote MCP server for AI clients, and the Pup CLI; authentication is via Datadog API/application keys, MCP OAuth, or Pup OAuth/stateless bearer auth depending on surface.
result.description: Datadog provides a cloud observability and security platform for monitoring infrastructure, applications, logs, traces, user experience, incidents, and related operational data. It also offers AI-oriented access paths such as a remote MCP server and an agent-friendly CLI for interacting with Datadog resources.

dd_api_key.label: Datadog API key
dd_api_key.setup: In Datadog, open [Organization Settings > API Keys](https://app.datadoghq.com/organization-settings/api-keys), click **New Key**, give it a name, and create the key. API keys are organization-scoped. Datadog docs state write requests require an API key.

dd_app_key.label: Datadog application key
dd_app_key.setup: In Datadog, open [Organization Settings > Application Keys](https://app.datadoghq.com/organization-settings/application-keys), click to create an application key, and copy the secret when shown. Application keys are associated with the creating user or service account; you can scope them to least privilege, and some APIs require enabling **Actions API access** on the key as described in the [API and Application Keys docs](https://docs.datadoghq.com/account_management/api-app-keys.md).

dd_pup_oauth.label: Datadog OAuth login for Pup CLI
dd_pup_oauth.setup: Install Pup, then run `pup auth login` as documented in the [Pup CLI docs](https://docs.datadoghq.com/cli.md). Pup opens a browser-based OAuth2/PKCE login flow and stores a scoped token for the CLI. For multi-org workflows you can use `pup auth login --org`.

dd_access_token.label: Datadog access token for stateless CLI auth
dd_access_token.setup: Use Datadog's token flow supported by Pup and provide the resulting bearer token in `DD_ACCESS_TOKEN`. The [Pup CLI docs](https://docs.datadoghq.com/cli.md) document `DD_ACCESS_TOKEN` as a bearer token for stateless authentication and note it can be used instead of `DD_API_KEY`/`DD_APP_KEY`. The easiest documented path for most users is still `pup auth login`; use this token form when you specifically need stateless or headless CLI auth.

dd_mcp_oauth.label: Datadog OAuth login for MCP server clients
dd_mcp_oauth.setup: Connect an MCP-capable client to Datadog and complete the OAuth login flow when prompted. Datadog documents this for ChatGPT, Claude connectors, Claude Code, Codex, and custom remote MCP clients in the [Datadog MCP Server setup docs](https://docs.datadoghq.com/mcp_server/setup.md). Use the MCP server endpoint for your Datadog site when manually configuring a remote connector.

variables.name: site
variables.description: Datadog site/region determines the API hostname; docs list examples including `api.datadoghq.com`, `api.us3.datadoghq.com`, `api.us5.datadoghq.com`, `api.datadoghq.eu`, `api.ap1.datadoghq.com`, and `api.ap2.datadoghq.com`.

variables.name: site
variables.description: Datadog site/region determines the remote MCP endpoint; docs instruct users to select their Datadog site and use the corresponding regional MCP server endpoint.
